recap: Angles on a line = 180, around a point = 360, in a triangle = 180. Alternate angles are equal; corresponding angles are equal; co-interior angles add to 180.

Q1.question: Angles on a straight line are 3x and 2x + 10. Find x.

Q2.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. An angle of 70 degrees is formed. Find the co-interior angle on the same side, giving a reason.

steps: Co-interior angles add up to 180 degrees (co-interior angles in parallel lines).
solution.final_answer: 110 degrees (co-interior angles in parallel lines add to 180)

Q3.question: A triangle has angles x, 2x - 15, and 3x + 9. Find all three angles.

steps: Angles in a triangle sum to 180: x + 2x - 15 + 3x + 9 = 180.

Q4.question: Find the size of each interior angle of a regular hexagon.

steps: Sum of interior angles = (6 - 2) x 180 = 4 x 180 = 720 degrees.

Q5.question: AB and CD are parallel lines. A transversal crosses them, making angle p with AB and angle q with CD (on the same side). Another transversal makes alternate angle r with AB. If p = 55 degrees, find q and r, giving reasons for each answer.

steps: q: p and q are co-interior angles (same-side interior angles between parallel lines), so p + q = 180. q = 180 - 55 = 125 degrees. Reason: co-interior angles in parallel lines.
r: p and r are alternate angles (on opposite sides of the transversal, between parallel lines), so r = p = 55 degrees. Reason: alternate angles in parallel lines are equal.
solution.final_answer: q = 125 degrees (co-interior angles); r = 55 degrees (alternate angles)

summary: Angles on a straight line sum to 180; angles at a point sum to 360; vertically opposite angles are equal.
In parallel lines: alternate angles are equal (Z); corresponding angles are equal (F); co-interior angles add to 180 (C).
Interior angle sum of a polygon = (n - 2) x 180; exterior angles always sum to 360.
Always state your reason in geometry — e.g. 'alternate angles in parallel lines'.
